hero.title: EAD
hero.subtitle: Evolutionary Autonomous Driving
hero.description: To bridge the technology gap in Korea's autonomous driving industry, SNU ADR Lab is releasing an End-to-End autonomous driving model and dataset designed for real-world deployment.

overview.sectionSubtitle: Academia meets industry to build Korea's autonomous driving baseline
overview.introLead: Releasing everything Korea's autonomous driving industry needs to get started.
introLines: Neither the lab alone nor companies alone can do it.
Academia handles the model and technology, while industry handles data and hardware.
What we build together will be released as a baseline for the entire Korean autonomous driving industry.

roadmap.sectionSubtitle: v1.0 designed for real-world deployment, v2.0 scaling with large-scale data

versions.title: Foundation
items: Training and validation on open-source datasets
Full pipeline: multi-task Perception & E2E Planning

dataset.sectionSubtitle: Trained and validated on both real-world and simulation data

realworld.title: Real-world Dataset
realworld.description: A dataset collected from real-world road environments. The model is trained and evaluated using the NAVSIM benchmark.

closedloop.title: Closed-loop Dataset
closedloop.description: A closed-loop evaluation dataset based on the CARLA simulator. The Bench2Drive benchmark provides evaluation quality equivalent to real driving environments.

inhouse.title: In-house Dataset
inhouse.description: Real-world driving data collected through our own data pipeline. We plan to build large-scale training data optimized for Korean road environments in collaboration with Alliance partners.

model.sectionSubtitle: An E2E autonomous driving model designed for real-world deployment

encoder.label: ENCODER
encoder.title: Perception
encoder.description: Integrates surround-view camera + LiDAR inputs to transform the surrounding environment into a structured representation.

decoder.label: DECODER
decoder.title: Planning
decoder.description: Simulates the future of candidate trajectories using a Sparse World Model, then selects the safest path through Safety Assessment.

notice.sectionSubtitle: Latest news from the EAD project

items.title: EAD v1.0 Release
items.content: We are releasing EAD v1.0, an E2E autonomous driving model designed for real-world deployment. Two configurations are available — Camera-Only and LiDAR-Camera — with training and validation completed on public benchmarks.

items.title: FMTC 2026 Presentation
items.content: The EAD project's vision and technical approach were presented at FMTC 2026.

contributors.sectionSubtitle: Research team at SNU ADR Lab

alliance.sectionSubtitle: Partners building Korea's autonomous driving ecosystem together with EAD

contact.collaborationTitle: Building Korea's Autonomous Driving Ecosystem Together
contact.collaborationNote:
We are looking for partners to build Korea's autonomous driving ecosystem together.
Model code requests, collaboration proposals — all inquiries are welcome.
